success_surface: Row deletion with dynamic target search in spreadsheet
effective_behaviors: Pre-manipulation workbook inspection to understand structure and locate targets
Programmatic target identification instead of assuming fixed positions
Reverse-order row deletion to prevent index shifting errors
Multi-method result verification combining direct cell access and file re-reading
Separate output path execution to preserve source file integrity
evidence: Agent examined sheet names and printed initial content before implementing deletion logic
Iterated through column cells using ws.cell() to find target marker position dynamically
Deleted rows using reverse iteration (range from rows_to_delete down to 1) to avoid shifting issues
Confirmed result via direct cell read AND re-loaded file with pandas for cross-validation
Saved modified workbook to distinct output path leaving original source untouched

memory_items.title: Workbook Structure Discovery
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure programmatically before making modifications
memory_items.generalization: List available sheets and preview relevant content areas to validate task scope and inform implementation strategy

memory_items.title: Dynamic Target Location
memory_items.lesson: Search for marker values through code iteration rather than hardcoded assumptions
memory_items.generalization: Use cell-by-cell scanning loops to locate header identifiers or keywords before performing dependent row/column operations

memory_items.title: Safe Row Deletion Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Delete rows in reverse index order to maintain stable cell references throughout the operation
memory_items.generalization: When removing multiple rows, process from highest index downward so earlier deletions don't shift remaining targets

memory_items.title: Dual Verification Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Confirm modifications through both direct workspace access and independent file re-reading
memory_items.generalization: Cross-check manipulated data using multiple methods to detect potential discrepancies or corruption

memory_items.title: File Preservation Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Direct all modifications to separate output locations to protect original data
memory_items.generalization: Never overwrite source files during experimentation; use distinct output paths for each save

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Workbook Discovery First
- Always list available sheets and preview relevant content before implementing modifications
- Use this information to validate task understanding and define scope boundaries

### Dynamic Target Search
- Locate marker values (headers, keys, identifiers) through programmatic scanning rather than assumptions
- Store found positions for subsequent operations like row or column manipulation

### Reverse Index Deletion
- When deleting multiple rows, iterate from highest to lowest index to prevent reference shifts
- This preserves correct targeting throughout the deletion sequence

### Cross-Method Validation
- Verify outcomes through at least two approaches: direct workspace checks AND re-reading saved files
- Confirm expected content placement and structural changes

### Output Safety
- Route all modifications to separate output files, never overwrite sources during development
- Preserve original file state for recovery and comparison purposes
